title: quercus × pongtungensis
intro: Quercus × pongtungensis, also known as the Pongtung oak, is a hybrid plant species within the Quercus genus, resulting from the cross-breeding of Quercus acutissima and Quercus serrata. The Pongtung oak has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Studies have shown that the plant extracts exhibit various health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and modulating immune responses. Quercus × pongtungensis is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and its herbal preparations are commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain diseases.
